- T001@8f31c2a7 [US1] Add `src/skills/dude-work/recovery.mjs` and `src/skills/dude-work/recovery.test.mjs` with the six-record contract; strict `parseInvocation(argv) -> {feature?,policy}` grammar preserving zero or one optional feature selector before flags; feature-only, Lightweight-task, and tracked issue-only Target variants with optional verified tracked task mapping; canonical target/evidence/approach/result hashes; and one canonical serialized model-facing packet capped at 16 available items and 65,536 UTF-8 bytes. Prove omitted versus supplied verified tracked `taskKey` yields the same `canonicalTarget`, model packet, and `evidenceHash` and cannot evade no-progress. Current-run normalization must cover `clarification-required`, `approval-required`, `external-dependency`, `safety-or-authority`, `blocked`, `failed`, `succeeded`, and `interrupted`; concrete functions alone derive status and blockers, and caller/model input cannot set authority, status, or blockers. Accept an issue-only tracked target before a durable key exists; whenever `taskKey` is supplied, verify its exact issue mapping. Verify with `node --test library/packs/beads/skills/dude-pack-beads-workflow/beads.test.mjs`.

Add no configurable limit system, new module, record, state, or parser framework.

# Tasks: Pre-work Log Learning

Reconciliation after independent review and simplification: kept 0, changed 0, dropped 11 open, new 8. The prior tasks were materially split, merged, or removed with the overbuilt engine, manifest, gate, and transaction design. No board, discovered work, archive, execution history, task state, or other execution evidence transfers.

Implementation is limited to exactly three modules: `src/skills/dude-work/recovery.mjs`, the existing `library/packs/beads/skills/dude-pack-beads-workflow/beads.mjs`, and `src/skills/dude-feature-definition/atomic-file-batch.mjs`. Tests are companions. Add no engine, adapter, manifest, gate, plugin, state store, or transaction schema.

Current re-definition after the second independent T008 rejection keeps T001-T007 byte-for-byte as completed historical evidence and changes only T008 in place. Recovery v1 is sequential and permits at most one pending authorization per invocation. This current package prose explicitly supersedes the completed T004 and T006 descriptions wherever they refer to parallel fan-out or multiple pending authorizations; those completed lines remain unchanged as historical execution evidence rather than the current contract.
